people.text: Amir wants cheap train tickets for a weekend trip. He needs to compare times and prices easily.
Lily is planning a walking holiday and wants maps showing routes, distances and difficulty levels.
Ben wants reviews of family hotels. He needs places with child-friendly facilities and nearby restaurants.
Eva wants ideas for short city visits. She prefers free museums and activities she can do in one day.
Jorge is travelling by bicycle and needs advice about safe roads and repair shops along the way.

texts.text: WalkMore offers downloadable maps with route length, expected time and [7]difficulty level[/7]. It is useful for travellers planning [7]walking holidays[/7] in the countryside.
Luxury Islands recommends expensive beach resorts and private boat trips for long holidays.
Cycle Roads shows quieter roads, steep sections and places where riders can fix problems. The map includes [10]bicycle repair shops[/10] and safety notes from other cyclists.
City Day Out suggests short routes for visitors who only have one day. It highlights [9]free museums[/9], parks and simple activities within walking distance of the centre.
Food Abroad lists famous restaurants but does not include transport or hotel advice.
Airport Lounge Guide reviews waiting areas inside international airports.
Rail Saver compares [6]ticket prices and departure times[/6] from several train companies. Users can quickly find the cheapest route for [6]weekend journeys[/6] and book through one page.
Family Stay Reviews focuses on hotels with children’s pools, play areas and large rooms. Parents comment on [8]child-friendly facilities[/8] and whether nearby restaurants suit families.

answers.explanation: Amir needs cheap train comparisons for a weekend trip.
Lily wants walking routes with distances and difficulty.
Ben needs family hotel reviews and facilities.
Eva wants free one-day city activities.
Jorge needs cycling safety and repair information.
